---
title: "【AI Daily】AI Brains at Your Doorstep: Siri Teams Up with Gemini as Robots and Chip Giants Clash"
date: "2026-03-30"
description: "The AI sector sees explosive progress: Japan is establishing an AI robot research center"
tags: ["AI", "Daily Report", "Tech Trends"]

---

### 📋 Quick Highlights
- Japan's government to establish an AI robot center, aiming for physical AI leadership.
- Apple partners with Google Gemini, making Siri smarter and more intuitive.
- Samsung plans to equip **800 million** devices with Gemini, popularizing AI experiences.
- NVIDIA's new chip platform drastically lowers AI inference costs, accelerating application deployment.
- The U.S. fast-tracks AI regulations, seeking a balance between innovation and oversight.
- LG Innotek collaborates with Applied Intuition, expanding from autonomous driving to robotics.
- SK hynix heavily invests in EUV equipment, ensuring global leadership in AI memory.

### What Happened?
Hey everyone! This morning, as soon as I opened the news, the AI world was practically exploding with advancements—it felt like a tech holiday party! First, I saw the Japanese government making a grand move to build an AI robot research center, clearly signaling their intent to reclaim the "robot king" throne. Then, I heard that Apple and Google are holding hands, with Gemini AI moving into Siri's "brain." This means our iPhones are about to get super smart! Not to mention Samsung's bold declaration to equip **800 million** devices with Gemini AI this year—that number alone is astounding! Chip giant NVIDIA wasn't idle either; with their new platform, the "brainpower cost" of AI has been drastically cut. It looks like the era of AI for all is truly not far off. All in all, today's AI news makes it feel like we're riding the crest of a technological wave, where every ripple could be a premonition of future life.

### Simply Put, What Is This?
If you imagine the entire AI ecosystem as a massive city, then today's big news is like a simultaneous major upgrade to the city's infrastructure, transportation system, and smart homes!

Imagine, **Japan's** new AI robot research center is like a huge investment in the city's "public service sector," aiming to make robot police, robot delivery personnel, and robot nurses smarter and more widespread, allowing the entire city to operate more efficiently.

Meanwhile, the collaboration between **Apple** and **Google Gemini** is like transforming your home's "smart assistant sidekick," Siri, from a task-oriented assistant into a truly "heart-to-heart" friend who understands your thoughts better. It won't just answer your questions but can also anticipate your needs based on context—it's essentially private secretary-level service! As for **Samsung's** plan to embed Gemini AI into **800 million** devices, it's like every household getting equipped with this "super helper," making AI transition from high-tech to everyday life.

As for **NVIDIA's** **Vera Rubin** platform, I see it as a revolutionary upgrade to the AI city's "power system," making previously energy-intensive AI computations fast and cost-effective. This means future AI applications will be as affordable and accessible as tap water, leading to the emergence of many more smart services we can't even imagine yet.

### According to Reports, Details Are as Follows
Today, the various heroes of the AI field are firing on all cylinders on their respective battlefronts, making the entire industry exceptionally vibrant.

#### Physical AI and Robotics: Japan and LG Innotek Make Their Moves 🤖
The **Japanese government** is playing for keeps this time! They announced the establishment of an **AI Robot Research Center**, aiming to create an international hub for R&D and talent development. This "AI Robot Strategy" is expected to be finalized soon, with the goal of accelerating the development and widespread adoption of autonomous AI robots in **16 sectors** (including logistics, nursing care, agriculture, disaster response, and national defense), projected for practical use by **2030**. After all, Japan commands a staggering **70%** market share in the global industrial robot market, and this offensive clearly aims to solidify its leading position in physical AI.

Concurrently, South Korean giant **LG Innotek** has signed a strategic partnership agreement with **Applied Intuition**, a global leader in autonomous driving software and simulation. This collaboration isn't limited to autonomous driving but extends its reach to "physical AI" domains like drones and robotics. LG Innotek will integrate its sensing modules (covering cameras, LiDAR, and radar) into Applied Intuition's test vehicles and simulation platforms, even utilizing "digital twin" technology to develop "virtual sensors." This will allow automakers to conduct development and validation in virtual environments, significantly shortening development cycles and seizing future market opportunities.

#### Consumer Electronics and AI Software: Apple, Samsung, Fujitsu Vie for Position 📱
The most talked-about news these past few days has been the "wedding of the century" between **Apple** and **Google**! During **WWDC 2026**, scheduled from **June 8th to 12th**, Apple is set to announce the deep integration of the **Google Gemini AI** model into Siri. This is no simple API connection; Apple reportedly has gained "unprecedented full access" to customize and refine Gemini technology for its next-generation Apple Intelligence features. A significantly upgraded and more personalized Siri is expected later this year. Future iOS 27 might even enable Siri to route queries to other AI assistants like **Claude** or **Perplexity**, fully unlocking Siri's potential.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-03-28-014713"
  title="AI Brains Evolve: Apple Teams with Google, ChatGPT-5.4 Arrives. Is Your Digital Life Ready?"
  description="In March 2026, the AI field saw multiple breakthroughs, including Apple and Google's deepened collaboration, integrating Gemini into Siri."
  author="科技白話文說書人"
  publisher="AI日報"
/>

Meanwhile, smartphone giant **Samsung's** ambition is even more astonishing. They have set a goal to double the cumulative installed base of AI-enabled Galaxy devices this year, reaching an incredible **800 million units**! The core of this strategy is to deploy Gemini AI on as many devices as possible to expand the AI user experience and strengthen user loyalty to the Samsung AI ecosystem.

At the same time, **Fujitsu** also launched a generative AI service in Japan on **March 30, 2026**, named "**Fujitsu Application Transform powered by Fujitsu Kozuchi**." This service leverages generative AI to analyze source code from legacy systems like COBOL and automatically generate design documents, aiming to assist enterprises in modernization and digital transformation.

#### AI Policy and Regulation: The U.S. and Global Regulatory Battle ⚖️
With the rapid advancement of AI technology, governments worldwide are also accelerating their pace in AI regulation. The U.S. federal government is fast-tracking the development of several AI-related regulations. The White House already released its "National AI Legislative Framework" on **March 20, 2026**, aiming to establish unified federal standards to address the increasingly fragmented regulatory landscape among states. Currently, over **1000 AI-related bills** have been introduced across all **50 U.S. states** and territories before 2025.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-03-27-015459"
  title="AI Free-for-All: Regulation, Agents, and Brain Shrinkage—Is Our Digital Life Still Safe?"
  description="Today's AI news focuses on the global wave of AI regulation, the rise of agentic AI systems and their ethical challenges, and major breakthroughs in AI hardware technology."
  author="科技白話文說書人"
  publisher="AI日報"
/>

This framework also highlights the deep interplay between federal and state powers, technological innovation, and public interest. One key challenge is how to address issues like "algorithmic bias" and "data transparency." More notably, **AI Deepfakes** have already begun to blur reality in the **2026 U.S. midterm elections**, such as an AI-generated advertisement in Texas making a politician appear to say things he never said, posing a severe test for democratic processes.

In Europe, obligations for General Purpose AI (GPAI) under the **EU AI Act** came into effect in **August 2025**, while comprehensive requirements for high-risk AI systems will be implemented in **August 2026**, even including energy efficiency reporting. This indicates a clear global trend toward AI regulation.

#### AI Chips and Model Technology: NVIDIA and SK hynix in an Arms Race ⚡
Behind the scenes of AI, breakthroughs in chip and model technology are the fundamental drivers of everything. **NVIDIA** unveiled its **Vera Rubin** platform at **GTC 2026**, the successor to Blackwell, which is now in production. It claims to boost inference throughput per watt by **10x** and reduce the cost per token by **one-tenth**. This means AI's "brain operations" will become cheaper and more efficient, significantly lowering the barrier for AI applications. NVIDIA even acquired **Groq's** inference technology for **$20 billion** and launched the **Groq 3** Language Processing Unit. This demonstrates that even the GPU titan recognizes that a single chip cannot meet all AI workload demands, highlighting the growing importance of inference-specific chips.

<StatsHighlight
  title="NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform Efficiency Boost"
  stats={[{value: "10x", label: "Inference throughput per watt increase"}, {value: "1/10", label: "Cost per token reduction"}]}
/>

On the other hand, memory giant **SK hynix** is aggressively strengthening its AI memory manufacturing capabilities. They have approved an investment of approximately **€6.9 billion (about $8 billion USD)** to purchase extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography systems from **ASML**, expected for delivery by the end of **2027**. This massive investment will expand EUV capacity for producing next-generation DRAM nodes (such as **1b**, **1c**) and High Bandwidth Memory (HBM), which are indispensable core components for AI accelerator systems. SK hynix even considers lithography technology the "master key" to becoming a "full-stack AI memory creator."

<Callout type="info" title="The Sora Lesson: The True Challenge of Operating Costs">
It's worth noting that OpenAI's Sora video generation platform announced its discontinuation on March 24. The reason was nothing less than exorbitant operating costs—up to **$15 million USD** per day, while total revenue was only **$2.1 million USD**. This stark contrast serves as a wake-up call for all companies pursuing cutting-edge AI technology: while technological breakthroughs are crucial, commercialization and cost control are equally vital for survival.
</Callout>

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-03-29-015816"
  title="AI Shake-up: Sora's Painful Lesson, GPT-5, Gemini 3.1 & the 'Real-World' Era of AI Agents Arrive!"
  description="OpenAI's Sora video generation platform announced its discontinuation due to exorbitant daily operating costs of $15 million and total revenue of only $2.1 million."
  author="科技白話文說書人"
  publisher="AI日報"
/>

### What Does This Mean for Us?
As an ordinary person, my first thought upon seeing this news is: "Wow, my digital life is about to be redefined again!"

First, a **smarter Siri** will definitely make our daily lives smoother. Imagine no longer "talking past each other" with Siri; it can truly understand your context. For example, if you say, "Book me dinner," it will automatically recommend restaurants based on your location, preferences, or even your recent conversations, instead of dumbly asking, "Which one?" It's like instantly training a clumsy intern into your dedicated life manager! And with Samsung's deployment across **800 million** devices, this intelligence will be ubiquitous, meaning AI will become an indispensable part of your life, no matter what phone you use.

Next, the development of **AI robots** will significantly change our society. The Japanese government's strategy suggests that more robots might enter public service in the future, easing the burden on human labor. For instance, in nursing homes, farms, or even disaster zones, robots will become invaluable helpers to humanity. While this will undoubtedly boost efficiency, it might also raise concerns about job displacement. However, I personally believe this is more about freeing us from highly repetitive and dangerous tasks, allowing us to pursue more creative and valuable endeavors.

As for the **reduction in AI chip costs**, for us developers and businesses, it's fantastic news! Previously, developing an AI application could be prohibitively expensive. Now, with lower costs, more startups and small teams can experiment with AI innovation, leading to a proliferation of fun and practical AI products and services. It's much like how, when the internet first emerged, lower server costs allowed all sorts of websites and apps to sprout up like mushrooms after rain.

However, every coin has two sides. The **accelerated advancement of AI regulations**, especially concerning deepfakes and algorithmic bias, makes me realize that while we enjoy the convenience of AI, we must also be wary of its potential risks. Just as drivers must follow traffic rules, AI needs a clear set of guidelines to steer its development in the right direction, protecting our privacy and rights.

<InsightQuote
  type="insight"
  content="The exponential growth in AI infrastructure investment is not merely technological progress; it's an economic and geopolitical contest. But without sound financial and risk management, this AI race could create the conditions for its own collapse."
  author="Jim Rickards"
  role="Former CIA Advisor"
/>

Former CIA Advisor **Jim Rickards'** warning also gives me pause. He suggests that this current frenzy of AI infrastructure development could become unsustainable due to changing market conditions, potentially even leading to a widespread economic collapse. This reminds us that AI development is not just a technological issue but also a matter of macroeconomic and financial stability.

### Insider's Deep Analysis
From the perspective of a seasoned investigative journalist, I believe today's AI news reveals several key industry trends and deeper commercial logic.

First, the **battleground for physical AI is fully opening up**. In the past, when we talked about AI, it was mostly about software, cloud, and algorithms. But now, giants like the Japanese government and LG Innotek are shifting their focus to "physical AI"—that is, truly bringing AI into the physical world, integrating it with robots, autonomous driving, and drones. This is not just a technological advancement but a shift in industry value: expanding from purely digital intelligence to the physical world, where it can generate tangible economic benefits and social impact. Japan's deep roots in industrial robotics give them the confidence to lead again, while LG Innotek's partnership with Applied Intuition showcases a trend of deep integration between hardware manufacturers and software platform providers. Future success will belong to players who can offer "integrated hardware-software solutions."

Second, **AI's "democratization" is moving from the cloud to the edge, and then directly into your hands**. Apple's integration of Gemini into Siri and Samsung's plan to equip **800 million** devices with Gemini clearly point to one direction: the intelligence of Large Language Models (LLMs) will no longer be solely the domain of cloud services. Instead, through optimization and compression, it will run directly on our personal devices like phones and tablets. The technical challenges behind this are immense; for instance, Google's **TurboQuant** algorithm can compress LLM memory requirements by **6x**, making "personalized AI" possible. This also means that **data privacy** will become an even more critical issue, as much personal data will be processed on-device rather than uploaded to the cloud, which is good news for users.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-03-26-015453"
  title="AI's Super Memory! Google TurboQuant Shrinks AI Brains by 6x. Are Chip Stocks Shaking? Agent AI Conquers Enterprises, Are You Still an AI Assistant?"
  description="The most explosive AI news is Google's research team launching the TurboQuant algorithm, capable of compressing large language model memory requirements by up to 6 times."
  author="科技白話文說書人"
  publisher="AI日報"
/>

Third, the **AI chip market is moving towards "specialized division of labor" and "cost optimization."** NVIDIA's **Vera Rubin** platform demonstrates that the demand for AI inference is exploding, potentially even surpassing the demand for training. NVIDIA's acquisition of Groq's technology and the launch of inference-specific units further illustrate that AI chips will no longer be "one-size-fits-all." Instead, more specialized and efficient hardware will be developed for different AI workloads (training, inference). Simultaneously, SK hynix's massive investment in EUV equipment ensures the stability of the AI memory (HBM) supply chain and technological leadership. This is an "arms race" that cannot be lost; whoever gains an advantage in HBM will hold sway in the AI era.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-03-19-015031"
  title="AI Battle Royale: Computing Power Surges, Robots Rise, AI Agents Vie for Jobs!"
  description="The AI field is tumultuous: global AI computing demand surges, leading to rising costs, while NVIDIA's GTC conference reveals new AI chip platforms and physical AI advancements."
  author="科技白話文說書人"
  publisher="AI日報"
/>

Finally, a **"federal-first" trend in AI regulation is emerging**. The U.S. White House has introduced a national AI legislative framework, attempting to unify the fragmented regulations across various states. Behind this are not only considerations for protecting innovation and avoiding fragmentation but also shadows of political maneuvering and geopolitical competition. Striking a balance between encouraging innovation and preventing risks will be a huge challenge for governments worldwide. The use of deepfakes in elections further highlights the importance of AI ethics and social responsibility.

Seeing these changes, I feel that the entire AI industry is like it has entered a rapidly developing "adolescence," full of infinite possibilities, but also accompanied by growth pains and challenges.

### One-Liner Summary
AI is no longer just a "genius holed up in the lab"; it's now stepping out to integrate into our lives, but only if we teach it how to "earn its keep" and "play by the rules"!

---
